headlines.title: CITY WATER WILL BE GIVEN ANALYSIS
headlines.subtitle: Commission Wants Inquiry to Determine Cause of Typhoid.
headlines.fullText: The Fort Worth board of health will cause a chemical analysis to be made of the city water in view of the nu- merous typhoid cases reported by City Physician Hayes. An order for the In- Nestiantion was passed by the city commission Tuesday upon the recont- mendation of Water Commissioner Blanke. Blanke said a report was in efretta- tion that the city water was respon- in the mains since May, 1913, and he ==== not the cause, Banke dem started an inquiry of his own but he would be more satisfactory. The board of health is to report Thursday. headlines.title: Mrs. Von Claussen Plans to Appeal Lunacy Conviction
headlines.fullText: Speriut to The Star-Teltgruan NEW YORK, Jan. 13 .- Mrs. Ida" von Claussen, famous because of her one- sided , controversy with ex-President Roosevelt, has announced through her attorneys that she will appeal the de- -t of general ses. sjons that declared her insane and sen- tenced her to Matteawan insane amy- lum. Mrs. von Claussen was tried on an Indictment charging her with sending Careme Chs Strauss, president of the board of water supply. busted toMe strauss from the Hotel Flora, Rome, Italy, last spring, and the writer threatened to "shoot him like a dog" unless he straightened out her divorce affairs so she could marry a London millionaire worth $50.000.000. Examining allenists testified that Mrs. von Claussen was suffering from progressive paranoia and that she might become violent and dangerous to the community at any moment. Mrs. von Claussen is a beautiful woman and of large physique.

headlines.title: LETTERS FROM PEOPLE
headlines.subtitle: Discussing Appointees.
headlines.fullText: Filltor Star-Telegram: In reply to Jan. 10. 1914. in which Commissioner Grant asserted that the trouble was the city didn't know what to do. that one pulled one way and two the other. the pawn evidence that the house is complete In divided acaller Itself and therefore it But the true source of the trouble lies in the people not appointing men to office of courage and business abil- Ity to manage the city's affairs. Men that can be relied upon not as theorists, but as financiers, would put the city in the foremost ranks of cities of the Union. But until politics is eliminated the people never will be awakened by that high sense of duty, and the call which is mighty, and must prevall. headlines.title: YOUNG WIFE'S ARM SHOT OFF BY MAN CLEANING SHOTGUN
headlines.fullText: Mrs. Robert Curran Jr. 18, wife of Robert Curran Jr. 1, head checker for day by the accidental discharge of a shotgun that her husband was clean- The necident occurred shortly be- fore size a. m. at the Curran feel- was sitting on the floor by his wife's side polishing the weapon. He did not know that it was loaded until the shell exploded. His wife's arm was shattered and several shot penetrated her side. Dr. G. W. Eastham ment the Injured woman to Johnson's sanitarium, where the arm was amputated. Mrs. Cur- ran's condition 5 precarious. The Currans were married a year ago last December.

headlines.title: AWARD CONTRACT FOR BRIDGE APPROACHES
headlines.subtitle: W. H. Martin's Low Bid for Making 20.000-Yard Fill Is $4,455.
headlines.fullText: W. H. Martin was awarded the con- tract for the building of angels Me brulee approaches late Mon- day by the court. His bid for making the 20,000- yard fill was $4,435, the lowest of fire Hubmitted. Tin other bids were: Martin Con- Montgomery & wyatt. $3,000.

headlines.title: $8,000 JUDGMENT IS LOST BY RAILROAD
headlines.fullText: T. K. Turner, former car inspector for the Texas & Pacific Railway Co. was given a Judgment for 14000 for Injuries received while in the employ er that company by a Jury in The trial began last would Turner was represented by Carlock & Carlock. Turner was hurt near Grand Prairie Aux. 1. 1912, when an embankment in a gravel pit caved in on him. He was seriously crushed and has not thor quelty recovered.
